title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Go Client Systems · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for https, tls identity, certificate trust, proxies, and downgrade boundaries, producing transport trust and certificate evidence record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http transport trust and certificate evidence record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Https Identity · changed case · Review Https Identity
steps.instruction:
review case — Go security handoff client connects to api3.internal.test through proxy.example.test using a private CA and optional client certificate. Verify requested identity, connected endpoint, roots, proxy authority, downgrade policy, and what evidence the browser lab cannot supply. Competency probe: Bind HTTPS authority to the requested host, certificate identity, authenticated transport, and application expectation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
